summary: Jeff Atwood celebrates 'five dollar words' in programming—uncommon terms with specialized meanings that provide moments of clarity when you discover them. Drawing from Eric Lippert's blog series, he explores three key concepts: idempotence (operations that produce the same result regardless of repetition), orthogonality (independent components that don't cause unexpected side effects), and immutability (values that cannot be changed after creation). Atwood argues that learning precise vocabulary for programming concepts represents a step toward mastery, transforming intuitive understanding into explicit knowledge. He invites readers to share their own favorite technical terms that marked milestones in their development as programmers.
key_points: Learning precise vocabulary for programming concepts is a step toward understanding and mastery
Idempotent functions return the same result whether called once or a thousand times, making them essential for caching
Orthogonality means components can be modified independently without unexpected side effects—like navigating north-south without affecting east-west position
Non-orthogonal systems create 'Whack-a-Mole' debugging where fixing one problem creates others elsewhere
Immutable objects like strings are never modified in place; operations create entirely new objects instead
money_quotes: Words you don't hear often, but also words that provide the thrill of discovery, that 'aha' moment as you realize a certain programming concept you knew only through experimentation and intuition has a name.
If modifying item #1 results in unexpected behavior in item #2, you have a major problem – that's a form of unwanted coupling.

We've all worked on systems where you make one small change over here, and another problem pops out over there. You constantly push them back – like that Whack-a-Mole game – and you just never finish.
There's nothing you can do to the number one that changes it. You cannot paint it purple, make it even or get it angry. It's the number one, it is eternal, implacable and unchanging.
Try to imagine your strings painstakingly carved out of enormous blocks of granite. Because they are – they're immutable!
Learning the right word to describe something you're doing is a small step on the road towards understanding and eventual mastery.
key_insight: Discovering precise technical vocabulary for programming concepts you already understand intuitively marks a milestone in your evolution as a developer.
